User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Moc-co English learning application, storing their basic profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification. (Assumes an external authentication system)

username.description: The display name chosen by the user.

registrationDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the user registered for the application.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a single English learning lesson in the Moc-co curriculum, comprising a story, questions, and grammar explanations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

title.description: The title of the lesson, e.g., 'The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Past Simple'.

description.description: A brief summary or overview of the lesson's content and objectives.

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the lesson (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

topic.description: The main topic or theme of the lesson (e.g., 'Daily Life', 'Travel', 'Grammar Focus').

lessonType.description: An array of types that categorize the lesson (e.g., 'Story', 'Grammar', 'Vocabulary').

audioUrl.description: URL to the audio file for the lesson's story or content, enabling listening practice.

pdfUrl.description: URL to a downloadable PDF file of the lesson for offline review and practice.

storyContentId.description: Reference to the StoryContent entity associated with this lesson. (Relationship: Lesson 1:1 StoryContent)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the lesson content was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the lesson content was last updated.

StoryContent.title: StoryContent
StoryContent.description: Stores the textual content of a story that is part of a lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StoryContent entity.

text.description: The full text of the story for the lesson.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Defines a single question for quizzes within a lesson, including multiple-choice options or short-answer details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this question belongs to. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N Question)

options.description: An array of possible answer options for multiple-choice questions.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer to the question. For multiple-choice, this should match one of the options.

explanation.description: An explanation provided to the user after they answer the question, detailing why an answer is correct or incorrect.

relatedGrammarPointId.description: Optional reference to a GrammarPoint entity if the question specifically tests a grammar rule. (Relationship: GrammarPoint 0/1:N Question)

GrammarPoint.title: GrammarPoint
GrammarPoint.description: Represents a specific grammar rule or concept explained within a lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GrammarPoint entity.

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson this grammar point belongs to. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N GrammarPoint)

title.description: The title or name of the grammar point (e.g., 'Past Simple Tense', 'Adverbs of Frequency').

explanation.description: Detailed explanation of the grammar rule, its usage, and nuances.

order.description: The sequential order of this grammar point within its lesson.

GrammarExample.title: GrammarExample
GrammarExample.description: Provides example sentences to illustrate a specific grammar point.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GrammarExample entity.

grammarPointId.description: Reference to the GrammarPoint this example illustrates. (Relationship: GrammarPoint 1:N GrammarExample)

exampleSentence.description: An example sentence demonstrating the grammar rule in practice.

translation.description: Optional translation of the example sentence to aid understanding.

UserLessonProgress.title: UserLessonProgress
UserLessonProgress.description: Tracks a specific user's progress through a particular lesson, including completion status and scores.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserLessonProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who is undertaking this lesson. (Relationship: User 1:N UserLessonProgress)

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson being progressed through. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N UserLessonProgress)

status.description: The current status of the user's progress for the lesson (e.g., 'started', 'in-progress', 'completed').

score.description: The overall score obtained by the user for the lesson's quizzes, typically a percentage.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user completed the lesson.

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last interaction with this specific lesson.

UserQuestionAttempt.title: UserQuestionAttempt
UserQuestionAttempt.description: Records a user's single attempt at answering a specific question within a lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserQuestionAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the attempt. (Relationship: User 1:N UserQuestionAttempt)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question that was attempted. (Relationship: Question 1:N UserQuestionAttempt)

userAnswer.description: The answer provided by the user for the question.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the user's answer was correct or incorrect.

attemptedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user attempted the question.

userLessonProgressId.description: Optional reference to the UserLessonProgress record to which this attempt contributes. (Relationship: UserLessonProgress 1:N UserQuestionAttempt)

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Access is strictly restricted to the authenticated user matching the 'userId' in the path (`request.auth.uid`). Achieves Authorization Independence via path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: A collection where the existence of a document (with `userId` as the document ID) grants administrative privileges. This supports DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) by using document existence for authorization, eliminating the need for custom claims or `get()` calls in security rules for admin checks. The document content can be minimal, serving purely as a marker.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: The main collection for all learning lessons. Documents are publicly readable to all authenticated users, enabling global lesson catalog browsing and lesson content consumption. Write access is restricted to administrators. Includes `audioUrl` and `pdfUrl` for listening and download, fulfilling user requirements. Supports QAPs by having a homogeneous public read security posture.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier for a lesson.

definition.description: Stores the detailed story text for each lesson. Documents are publicly readable, inheriting the security posture of the parent lesson. Write access is restricted to administrators. Authorization Independence is maintained as `lessonId` is present in the path.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: storyContentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the story content of a specific lesson.

definition.description: Contains all questions for a specific lesson. Documents are publicly readable for users to attempt, ensuring QAPs for lesson quizzes. Write access is restricted to administrators. Authorization Independence is maintained as `lessonId` is present in the path.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a question within a lesson.

definition.description: Details specific grammar rules for each lesson. Documents are publicly readable, offering grammar explanations to all users. Write access is restricted to administrators. Authorization Independence is maintained as `lessonId` is present in the path.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: grammarPointId
params.description: The unique identifier for a grammar point within a lesson.

definition.description: Provides example sentences for specific grammar points. Documents are publicly readable, inheriting the security from parent grammar points. Write access is restricted to administrators. Authorization Independence is maintained as `lessonId` and `grammarPointId` are present in the path.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson.

params.name: grammarPointId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent grammar point.

params.name: grammarExampleId
params.description: The unique identifier for an example sentence.

definition.description: Tracks the progress of a specific user for each lesson. Documents are private, accessible only by the `userId` specified in the path (`request.auth.uid`). The `lessonId` is used as the document ID for a 1:1 user-to-lesson progress mapping. Authorization Independence is achieved through path-based ownership. QAPs are supported for listing a user's progress.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the lesson being progressed, serving as the document ID for progress.

definition.description: Records individual question attempts made by a user within a specific lesson. Documents are private, accessible only by the `userId` in the path. This nested structure ensures all user-specific attempts are tied to their overall lesson progress. Authorization Independence is achieved through path-based ownership. QAPs are supported for listing attempts within a user's lesson progress.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who made the attempt, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent lesson for which the attempt was made.

params.name: userQuestionAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific user's attempt at a question.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure for the Moc-co English learning application prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability in alignment with the core design principles and mandates. The structure clearly separates publicly accessible educational content from private user-specific progress data and leverages path-based authorization and existence checks for role management.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This design strictly avoids `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules for authorization. This is achieved through two primary strategies:
1.  **Public Content Collections:** Lessons, story content, questions, grammar points, and grammar examples are stored in top-level or sub-collections that are publicly readable by all authenticated users (`/lessons/{lessonId}`, `/lessons/{lessonId}/storyContent/{storyContentId}`, etc.). Since the entire collection or sub-collection has a homogeneous 'public read' security posture, no complex parent document lookups are needed to determine access rights for individual documents within them.
2.  **Path-Based User Ownership:** Private user data, such as user profiles, lesson progress, and question attempts, is strictly organized under a user's UID (`/users/{userId}`, `/users/{userId}/lessonProgress/{lessonId}`, `/users/{userId}/lessonProgress/{lessonId}/questionAttempts/{userQuestionAttemptId}`). This places the `userId` directly into the document's path, allowing security rules to directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` wildcard. This eliminates any need to `get()` a parent `User` document to verify ownership.
3.  **DBAC for Admin Roles:** Administrative privileges are managed via a dedicated `adminUsers/{userId}` collection. Authorization rules check for the *existence* of a document at `/adminUsers/$(request.auth.uid)`. This is a direct check against `request.auth.uid` and does not involve `get()` calls to traverse a data hierarchy.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure ensures that `list` operations are secure and efficient:
*   Listing `lessons`, `lessons/{lessonId}/questions`, etc., is secure because all documents in these collections are explicitly public-read. Rules simply grant `read` access to all documents.
*   Listing `users/{userId}/lessonProgress` or `users/{userId}/lessonProgress/{lessonId}/questionAttempts` is secure because the `userId` in the path ensures that a user can only query their own data (`request.auth.uid == userId`). The security rules filter by path, not by document content, guaranteeing a safe and efficient `list` operation without accidental data exposure.

**Structural Segregation:** Public content (lessons and their components) is kept entirely separate from private user data (progress tracking). This ensures homogeneous security requirements within each collection, simplifying rule writing and understanding.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Public Data:** `lessons`, `storyContent`, `questions`, `grammarPoints`, `examples` are public read, admin write.
*   **Private User Data:** `users`, `lessonProgress`, `questionAttempts` are path-based, owned by the authenticated user.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** `adminUsers` uses existence for role assignment.

This design effectively addresses the user's requirements for audio listening and PDF downloads by making the `Lesson` documents, containing `audioUrl` and `pdfUrl`, publicly accessible. The nested structure for user progress ensures detailed tracking of lesson completion and question attempts while strictly adhering to user data privacy.
